$description: Button component tokens. Third layer above primitives and semantics — maps semantic color tokens to specific button roles and states. Use these in UI code instead of semantic tokens directly, so button styles can be updated system-wide without touching components.

button.$description: Button variants: primary (one per page, main CTA), secondary (supporting actions), ghost (chromeless/tertiary), danger (destructive). Each variant defines default and hover states.

primary.$description: Primary button — filled, high-emphasis. One per page maximum.

default.$description: Filled action color — blue in decision-engine, phosphor green in base theme.

active.$description: Pressed/active state — deep darkened action color.

default.$description: Text/icon color on filled action background — always white in decision-engine.
Matches background so no visible border seam.

secondary.$description: Secondary button — outlined, medium-emphasis. Multiple per page allowed.

default.$description: White canvas background — the button sits flush with the page surface.

hover.$description: Subtle gray lift on hover — signals interactivity without implying primary priority.

default.$description: Near-black text — neutral, not action-colored. Blue is reserved for interactive links and primary actions.
Standard border — same gray used for inputs and cards.

hover.$description: Border unchanged on hover — background shift is the only state signal.

ghost.$description: Ghost button — transparent fill and no border, low-emphasis. Neutral text with a subtle surface tint on hover. Use for tertiary actions, toolbars, and dense UI.

default.$description: No fill — ghost buttons are chromeless at rest, sitting flush on any surface.

hover.$description: Subtle surface tint on hover — the resting-state affordance for an otherwise chromeless button. Same lift as secondary's hover.

default.$description: Neutral primary text — ghost buttons never use accent green as resting text.

hover.$description: No border on hover — the background tint is the only state signal.

danger.$description: Danger button — for destructive or irreversible actions (delete, remove).

default.$description: White — danger button is outlined by default, not filled.

hover.$description: Subtle red tint on hover signals destructive intent.

default.$description: Red text communicates the destructive nature of the action.
Red border reinforces the danger signal.
